title: Durable Due Scheduling, Clock Injection, Timers, Backoff, and Pause · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs durable due scheduling, clock injection, timers, backoff, and pause from source and emitted revision, tenant, URL, validator, bytes, runtime validation, Promise settlement, transaction, lease, response, failure, handle cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for durable handle-clean adaptive scheduler after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher guidance, pause cause, and schedule reason outside process timers.
Inject clock and sleep ports, distinguish elapsed from civil time, define boundary equality, and test skew and jumps.
Schedule the next bounded timer only after ownership and settlement, unref deliberately, clear on close, and expose handle evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

stimulus.title: Feed Ts Pause Resume Policy case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 5868 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove durable due state, injected clocks, non-overlapping timers, backoff, pause, and handle cleanup; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model manual, policy, repeated-failure, incident, deletion, and maintenance pauses with authorization, evidence, and safe resume.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Removing a timer fully explains why a source is paused.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model manual, policy, repeated-failure, incident, deletion, and maintenance pauses with authorization, evidence, and safe resume. Artifact under review: durable handle-clean adaptive scheduler.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Feed Ts Pause Resume Policy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Feed Ts Pause Resume Policy
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Ts Pause Resume Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review TypeScript team handles case 7487 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove durable due state, injected clocks, non-overlapping timers, backoff, pause, and handle cleanup; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model manual, policy, repeated-failure, incident, deletion, and maintenance pauses with authorization, evidence, and safe resume.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Feed Ts Pause Resume Policy, keep Feed Ts Pause Resume Policy connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model manual, policy, repeated-failure, incident, deletion, and maintenance pauses with authorization, evidence, and safe resume.
